title: Forest of Numbers
description: Master aggregations: count, sum, average, groupBy — and learn to filter on aggregated results.

theory_scroll.content: Dungeon II covers the operations that move or reshape data across the cluster — joins, window functions, unions, UDFs, and caching. These are where most Spark performance issues live at MAANG scale, and where junior engineers make the most expensive mistakes.

rows: Skew on join key use broadcast() or salting
No PARTITION BY = full data to 1 executor = OOM
unionByName() vs union() by position — silent corruption risk
Always prefer built-in functions; use pandas_udf if must
Lazy — must run action to materialize. Always unpersist() when done

theory_scroll.content: The #1 production incident cause at MAANG data teams: data skew on join keys (especially null/unknown values). Always check key distribution with df.groupBy(key).count().orderBy('count', ascending=False).show(10) before joining.
